message:
Add browser reactor scaffold and browser-compatible entropy source

Introduce a BrowserReactor implementation for wasm32 targets and a
BrowserEntropy source backed by Web Crypto, completing the platform
adapter layer needed for browser runtime construction.

src/runtime/reactor/browser.rs (new, 327 lines):
- Add BrowserReactorConfig with max_wakers and max_pending_events
- Implement BrowserReactor providing the Reactor trait using an
  in-memory waker slab and pending-event queue, suitable for browser
  environments where epoll/kqueue are unavailable
- Implement register/deregister/modify/wake/poll with proper
  unknown-token handling (diagnostic, non-panicking)
- Default creates a reactor with 1024 max wakers and 256 pending events

src/runtime/reactor/mod.rs:
- Add browser module and re-export BrowserReactor + BrowserReactorConfig
- Add wasm32-targeted create_reactor() returning BrowserReactor
- Add wasm32 to the platform exclusion guard for the unsupported-
  platform fallback
- Add reactor_trait_bounds_browser and cross_reactor_compliance_browser
  tests verifying the browser reactor satisfies all Reactor trait
  contracts including empty-state, wake, nonblocking poll, deregister-
  unknown, and modify-unknown compliance checks

src/runtime/mod.rs:
- Re-export BrowserReactor and BrowserReactorConfig from reactor module

src/util/entropy.rs:
- Add BrowserEntropy struct implementing EntropySource, backed by
  getrandom (which maps to crypto.getRandomValues on wasm32)
- Intentionally a distinct type from OsEntropy so browser-specific
  entropy policies can be added without affecting native paths

src/util/mod.rs:
- Re-export BrowserEntropy

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
